title: Generator Start Failure
exclusions: Does not replace vendor start-circuit wiring inspections, NFPA 110 witness tests, or relay coordination studies‚ÄĒonly operationalizes telemetry already modeled as `generator:events` with the fields this UC names.
evidence: Retain the exported alert artifact with `_time`, `generator_id`, `start_result`, `crank_count`, and `fault_code`, add the correlating SNMP walk or controller HMI screenshot, and link the CMMS work order or EPS test sheet ID in the incident record for auditors.

controlTest.positiveScenario: During a supervised emergency power systems test the utility feed is opened, GEN-PLT-03 records `start_result=fail`, `crank_count=4`, and `fault_code=CRANK_TIMEOUT` within one poll, the saved search returns the row inside the five-minute window, and the alert action delivers a notification to the facilities queue with the attached table screenshot.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Field networking pauses SNMP walks for twenty minutes while the generator never actually cranks; stale cached `generator:events` replay old `fail` rows but `_indextime` jumps more than fifteen minutes ahead of `_time`, so the guard macro drops the replay and the alert remains quiet until a contemporaneous poll arrives.

dataSources: Land operational start and fault rows on **`index=power`** as **`sourcetype="generator:events"`** with Splunk-time extractions for **`start_result`**, integer **`crank_count`**, textual **`fault_code`**, and asset key **`generator_id`** (match your `props.conf`/`transforms.conf` aliases to the vendor‚Äôs engine log or BMS-forwarded CSV so the catalog SPL predicates stay true). Typical collection pairs a **Heavy Forwarder** running **Splunk Connect for SNMP ([Splunkbase app 6266](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6266))** or the **SNMP Modular Input add-on ([Splunkbase app 1537](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1537))** against the generator controller‚Äôs enterprise MIB table, syslog/HEC pushes from the **Generator controller** integration, or historian replays‚ÄĒwhichever path your facility team certifies.
spl:
index=power sourcetype="generator:events"
| search start_result IN ("fail","abort") OR crank_count > 3
| table _time, generator_id, start_result, crank_count, fault_code
| sort -_time
description: Detects generator-controller start telemetry on `sourcetype="generator:events"` where `start_result` reports a failed or aborted sequence, where `crank_count` climbs past the site‚Äôs allowed attempts, or where a non-empty `fault_code` pairs with those states before the alternator confirms stable output‚ÄĒpatterns that indicate the engine may never close onto the emergency bus.
value: When utility power blinks or fails, operators need proof the diesel can take the load quickly; lingering start faults burn finite UPS reserve, compress the window for orderly IT shutdowns, and can strand mechanical plant (chillers, pumps) without a credible backup source, so flagging failed cranks early drives disciplined maintenance and clearer customer-communications decisions.
implementation: Normalize every start attempt with `start_result`, `crank_count`, `fault_code`, and `generator_id` on `generator:events`, align `_time` to controller clock, schedule the detection on a cadence that sees at least two post-start polls, correlate hits with CMMS exercises, and throttle notifications when maintenance lookups mark a host in a sanctioned test.
visualization: Primary **`splunk.table`** sorted on `_time` showing `generator_id`, `start_result`, `crank_count`, `fault_code`; companion **`splunk.singlevalue`** counting distinct failed starts over **90d**; **`splunk.line`** trending `crank_count` maxima by `generator_id`; optional **`splunk.markdown`** panel listing CMMS suppression lookup rules for operators.

references.title: Splunk Documentation ‚ÄĒ Create and manage scheduled alerts

knownFalsePositives:
‚ÄĘ **Monthly loaded generator exercise (CRANK-EX-30)** ‚ÄĒ Maintenance teams expect several cranks before load is applied; distinguish by joining a **`facility_gen_exercise`** lookup keyed on `generator_id` and suppress inside the published change window while still indexing the rows.
‚ÄĘ **Battery-paralleling UPS software test** ‚ÄĒ Simulated mains loss makes the controller log `abort` lines with benign `fault_code` enumerations (`ABORT_OPERATOR`); distinguish by requiring `crank_count` above the documented site policy OR by filtering out fault codes whitelisted in **`props`**.
‚ÄĘ **SNMP poller cold start after Splunk upgrades** ‚ÄĒ **SNMP Modular Input (1537)** workers replay stale engine rows with identical `crank_count`; distinguish by dropping duplicates where `_indextime-_time` exceeds **600** seconds unless a fresh poll corroborates.
‚ÄĘ **BMS historian backfill batches** ‚ÄĒ Overnight catch-up imports duplicate historical `fail` markers; distinguish by enforcing `dedup generator_id crank_count fault_code` within a sliding **60**-second bucket or route backfill hosts to a lower-priority alert queue.
equipmentModels: Generator controller start-event normalization (facility SNMP or HEC feed)
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚ÄĘ Stand up ingestion for the **Generator controller** feed your runbook names: SNMP walks or traps via **Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase 6266)** or **SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase 1537)** on a Heavy Forwarder with `inputs.conf` stanzas pointing at the management VLAN, or validated **HEC** (`/services/collector/event`) streams from the building-automation historian‚ÄĒnever embed community strings in SPL.
‚ÄĘ Contract `props.conf` / `transforms.conf` so rows land on **`index=power`** as **`sourcetype="generator:events"`** with reliable fields **`start_result`**, **`crank_count`**, **`fault_code`**, and **`generator_id`** before scheduling detections.
‚ÄĘ Create RBAC such as **`power_gen_ops`** limited to **`index=power`** for human analysts and delegate **`schedule_search`** to a **`svc_facility_alerts`** service account that cannot read security indexes.

Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Configure data collection
Poll or stream start/fault logs at least every **five minutes** (`cron` `*/5 * * * *` on SNMP jobs) and document ENUM mappings for **`start_result`** values your vendor emits (`fail`, `abort`, etc.); stamp CMMS-derived maintenance windows into a lookup so exercises can be suppressed without losing raw events.

Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Create the search and alert
Paste the UC SPL into **Search & Reporting**, validate row counts over **`-24h@h`**, then save as an alert whose **`dispatch.earliest_time`** covers two poll intervals (for example **`-15m`**) while **`dispatch.latest_time`** stays **`now`**; wire actions to PagerDuty/email per your DR plan.

Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Validate
Compare each triggered **`generator_id`** against the controller‚Äôs front-panel or vendor web UI for the same window, reconcile **`crank_count`** stair-steps with waveform captures if available, and prove `_time` skew versus **NTP** stays under one poll period.

Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize & troubleshoot
Publish a dashboard pairing the failure table with a ninety-day **`splunk.singlevalue`** of distinct failed starts, document runbook tasks (battery/bost, fuel solenoid, air-bleed), and if **`_indextime`** lags chronically, treat it as forwarder backlog before declaring a mechanical failure.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
